title: pouteria chiricana
intro: Pouteria chiricana, also known as Guanacaste, is a plant species native to Central America, particularly in the tropical forests of Costa Rica and Panama. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Pouteria chiricana include the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as an antiseptic for wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, Pouteria chiricana is often used in infusions, teas, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to the skin in the form of creams and ointments.
